summary: Tail-recursive lockstep accumulation of sum AND product over an i64 slice (sum_product_at); a single combined strong-induction invariant on `size - i.toNat` proves both component postconditions simultaneously — signed multiplication overflow handled via BitVec.smulOverflow + Int64.mulOverflow_iff (parallel to the addition treatment), dual prefix-Int specs (sum_of_int, product_of_int) threaded in lockstep with two `hfit` preconditions, and the tuple result destructured into three top-level theorems (empty-slice boundary, sum-component, product-component) each specialising the same invariant at i=0,sum=0,product=1.
